steps.title: Find the interior angle at C using the exterior angle
steps.content:
The angle marked $110^{\circ}$ is an exterior angle at point C. Since angles on a straight line sum to $180^{\circ}$, the interior angle ∠BCA is supplementary to the exterior angle:
$$\angle BCA = 180^{\circ} - 110^{\circ} = 70^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Use the isosceles triangle property
steps.content:
We're told that ∠BAC = ∠BCA. Since ∠BCA = $70^{\circ}$, we have:
$$\angle BAC = 70^{\circ}$$
This means triangle ABC is isosceles with the two base angles equal.

steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum
steps.content:
The three angles of triangle ABC must sum to $180^{\circ}$:
$$\angle BAC + \angle BCA + \angle ABC = 180^{\circ}$$
$$70^{\circ} + 70^{\circ} + x = 180^{\circ}$$
$$x = 40^{\circ}$$
D is correct.
